meta.ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances
meta.project_subject: Setting up of PMU for CPGRAMS with 3 resource persons for a period beyond 01-04-26
meta.deliverables: The PMU will prepare monthly multidimensional reports on public grievances received on CPGRAMS by the 5th of each month (last acceptable date 10th). They will review and streamline existing categorization/taxonomy and mapping across remaining Central Ministries/Departments. They will conduct root cause analysis reviews covering grievances, disposal, and feedback for all Ministries/Departments. They will study the Public Grievances System for select Ministries/Departments and States/UTs to understand grievance flow and bottlenecks. They will publish an Annual Report on CPGRAMS by the 2nd/3rd week of December each year. They will make weekly and monthly presentations regarding CPGRAMS review at Secretary, DARPG level and other higher authorities as desired. They will assist DARPG in publicity & communication strategy for CPGRAMS and prepare reports/presentations/IEC material including Annual Report of the Department, INDIA Year Book, Year End Review by Hon'ble MoS(PP)/Secretary. They will assist in rollout of Next Gen CPGRAMS and Chatbot coordination. They will perform any other work assigned by DARPG from time to time.

content.full_text: The Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG), Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Government of India, issued Work Order S-15/6/2026-PG-DARPG(C.No.9934) on an unspecified date in March 2026. The order extends an existing arrangement with Quality Council of India (QCI) to set up a Project Management Unit (PMU) with three resource persons for 9 months from 01-04-2026 to 31-12-2026 or until implementation of NextGen CPGRAMS, whichever is earlier, at a cost of Rs.53,52,480/- including taxes. The PMU's responsibilities include preparing monthly multidimensional reports on CPGRAMS public grievances by the 5th of each month, reviewing and streamlining categorization across Central Ministries/Departments, conducting root cause analysis studies, publishing an Annual Report by December, making weekly/monthly presentations to DARPG authorities, assisting with publicity and communication strategies, supporting Next Gen CPGRAMS rollout, and performing other assigned tasks. Payment is scheduled in five 20% milestones at 2, 4, 6, 8, and 9 months completion. The order was signed by Garima Yadav, Deputy Secretary.
